perturbation.original_text: 1. GRANT OF RIGHTS: (a) Wireless Products and Wireless Platform: Fox grants to Licensee a limited, exclusive (except as otherwise may be provided in this Agreement), non-transferable (except as permitted in Paragraph 17(d)) right and license to use, make, have made (as set forth in Paragraph 1(a)(i) below), reproduce, modify, and create derivative works of Wireless Content License Agreement Multiple Properties / Sorrent, Inc. / Final PLZ ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. -1- Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 the PSM in each Property, solely for the purpose of developing the wireless applications specifically set forth for each of the Properties on Exhibits B through M attached hereto ("Wireless Products"), some of which Wireless Products are defined in the Glossary attached hereto as Exhibit N.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. GRANT OF RIGHTS: (a) Wireless Products and Wireless Platform: Fox grants to Licensee a limited, non-exclusive right and license to use, make, have made (as set forth in Paragraph 1(a)(i) below), reproduce, modify, and create derivative works of Wireless Content License Agreement Multiple Properties / Sorrent, Inc. / Final PLZ ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. -1- Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 the PSM in each Property, solely for the purpose of developing the wireless applications specifically set forth for each of the Properties on Exhibits B through M attached hereto ("Wireless Products"), some of which Wireless Products are defined in the Glossary attached hereto as Exhibit N.
perturbation.explanation: The term "exclusive" is a key term defining the scope of the rights granted to the Licensee. Changing the term to "non-exclusive" directly contradicts the initial grant of rights, implying that Fox could license these rights to other parties as well. This creates uncertainty in enforcement as it weakens the Licensee's control over the Wireless Products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) Fox's Reservation of Rights: Fox expressly reserves and retains any and all rights that are expressly excluded from or not specifically granted to Licensee pursuant to this Agreement, including but not limited to the following: (i) Development/Exploitation/Distribution: the right to develop, publish, and/or distribute (or grant a third party the right to develop, publish, and/or distribute) other wireless products or wireless content of any nature which may be derived from the Properties (and further, to exploit same) which are similar or in direct Wireless Content License Agreement Multiple Properties /Sorrent, Inc. / Final PLZ -3- Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 competition with the Wireless Products at any time in Japan including during the Term and within Japan, provided that Fox will not grant to any other party any such rights to develop, publish, and/or distribute Wireless Products for the Wireless Platform to end users outside of Japan (with the exception of those distribution rights for Wireless Products granted to VGSL under the VGSL Agreement in accordance with Paragraph 1(f) of this Agreement), or provide to any Licensee Competitor (as defined in Exhibit Q) any Licensee Materials or otherwise grant any rights to any Licensee Competitor to develop, publish, and/or distribute Wireless Products, or other wireless products or content based on the Properties. Fox has not granted and will not grant any exclusive distribution rights with respect to the Wireless Products to VGSL or any other party; and
perturbation.justification: The original text granted an "exclusive" right, while the changed text grants a "non-exclusive" right. This directly contradicts section (e) which states "Fox will not grant to any other party any such rights to develop, publish, and/or distribute Wireless Products for the Wireless Platform to end users outside of Japan". By changing "exclusive" to "non-exclusive", Fox is now free to grant other parties the same rights, directly conflicting with the previous reservation of rights and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. TERRITORY: Licensee shall use commercially reasonable efforts to exploit the rights granted hereunder throughout the world (the "Territory"), including without limitation all of the following VGSL Territories set forth in Paragraph 2(c)(i)(B) above: (1) United Kingdom; (2) Ireland; (3) Germany; (4) Spain; (5) France; (6) Sweden; (7) Switzerland; (8) Portugal; (9) Netherlands; (10) Greece; (11) Italy; (12) Australia; (13) New Zealand; (14) Egypt; (15) Slovenia; (16) Belgium; (17) Austria; (18) Hungary; (19) Malta; (20) Croatia; (21) South Africa; and (22) Japan. Licensee shall localize the Wireless Products into the following languages: English, French, German, Italian, Spanish, and Japanese. In addition to the previously stated languages, to the extent practicable Licensee also shall localize the Wireless Products to be delivered to VGSL into Portuguese, Greek, Dutch and Swedish, to the extent that the Properties Wireless Content License Agreement Multiple Properties / Sorrent, Inc. / Final PLZ -8- Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 are dubbed into such languages, Fox holds foreign rights to the dubbed tracks, and such dubbed tracks are timely available.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. TERRITORY: Licensee shall use commercially reasonable efforts to exploit the rights granted hereunder throughout the world (the "Territory"), excluding Japan. Licensee shall localize the Wireless Products into the following languages: English, French, German, Italian, and Spanish. In addition to the previously stated languages, to the extent practicable Licensee also shall localize the Wireless Products to be delivered to VGSL into Portuguese, Greek, Dutch and Swedish, to the extent that the Properties Wireless Content License Agreement Multiple Properties / Sorrent, Inc. / Final PLZ -8- Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 are dubbed into such languages, Fox holds foreign rights to the dubbed tracks, and such dubbed tracks are timely available.
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly excluding Japan from the definition of "Territory" and omitting Japanese from the required languages, this change contradicts other sections that grant Licensee rights and responsibilities related to Japan. This creates uncertainty over which terms apply to Japan, potentially leading to disputes over distribution and royalty payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (iii) Japan Distribution: Upon Fox's notice to Licensee, Licensee shall cease immediately some or all distribution of the Wireless Products in Japan. In the event Fox provides such termination notice to Licensee, Licensee hereby grants Fox a license to the Licensee Materials in order for Fox to distribute the Wireless Products that Licensee has developed for distribution in Japan prior to such termination, either directly or through a third-party. In consideration of such license, Fox shall pay Licensee a royalty as set forth in Paragraph 7(b)(i) below.
perturbation.justification: The original text included Japan within the 'Territory' and listed Japanese as a required language for localization. The changed text explicitly excludes Japan from the 'Territory' and removes Japanese from the required languages. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which outlines specific obligations and rights related to distribution in Japan and royalty payments. By excluding Japan, the changed text creates a conflict regarding the enforceability of the 'Contradicted Text', specifically paragraph (iii) Japan Distribution, as it is unclear whether those obligations still apply. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. GUARANTEE: (a) Amount: Licensee shall pay to Fox a non-refundable guarantee in the amount of US$***** ("Guarantee"), inclusive of the Advance. The Guarantee shall be recoupable against royalty payments and payable as follows: (a) US$***** inclusive of the Advance above; (b) US$***** on or before *****; (c) US$***** on or before *****; (d) US$***** on or before *****; (e) US$***** on or before *****; (f) US$***** on or before *****; and (g) US$***** on or before *****. There shall be no cross-collateralization among the Royalties earned, as defined in Paragraph 7 below, for the purposes of recouping the Guarantee for any of the Major Releases or Targeted Releases.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. GUARANTEE: (a) Amount: Licensee shall pay to Fox a refundable guarantee in the amount of US$***** ("Guarantee"), inclusive of the Advance. The Guarantee shall be recoupable against royalty payments and payable as follows: (a) US$***** inclusive of the Advance above; (b) US$***** on or before *****; (c) US$***** on or before *****; (d) US$***** on or before *****; (e) US$***** on or before *****; (f) US$***** on or before *****; and (g) US$***** on or before *****. There shall be no cross-collateralization among the Royalties earned, as defined in Paragraph 7 below, for the purposes of recouping the Guarantee for any of the Major Releases or Targeted Releases.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the Guarantee from “non-refundable” to “refundable” creates a direct contradiction. This creates uncertainty about whether the Licensee is entitled to a refund of the Guarantee under certain circumstances. This change directly creates an in-text contradiction within the contract on the true nature and terms of the guarantee being issued.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. ADVANCE: Licensee shall pay Fox a non-refundable recoupable advance in the amount of US$***** upon Licensee's signature of this Agreement (the "Advance"). The Advance shall be treated as the first installment of the Guarantee described in Section 6(a), and will be recoupable against royalties as part of the Guarantee.
perturbation.justification: The change from "non-refundable guarantee" to "refundable guarantee" in Section 6(a) directly contradicts the statement in Section 5 that the advance, which is treated as the first installment of the guarantee, is "non-refundable". This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the refundability of the guarantee and its first installment, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.
